articles.title: Microsoft and OpenAI’s famed AGI agreement is dead
articles.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have ended their AGI clause agreement, loosening their partnership. OpenAI can now serve products on any cloud provider, though Microsoft remains the primary partner.

articles.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face off in a high-stakes trial beginning April 27th. Musk sues OpenAI for abandoning its nonprofit mission and prioritizing profits over AI's humanitarian purpose.

articles.title: Canva apologizes after its AI tool replaces ‘Palestine’ in designs
articles.summary: Canva's Magic Layers AI tool was caught replacing the word 'Palestine' with 'Ukraine' in user designs. The company apologized after the feature, meant to edit image components, made unintended visible alterations.

articles.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
articles.summary: Skye's AI-powered iPhone home screen app secured investor backing before launch, signaling growing market interest in AI-enhanced mobile experiences.

articles.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
articles.summary: China blocked Meta's $2 billion Manus acquisition after a lengthy investigation, potentially hindering Mark Zuckerberg's artificial intelligence agent development plans.

articles.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
articles.summary: OpenAI is reportedly developing an AI phone where intelligent agents replace traditional apps. The device could enter mass production by 2028.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS. The funding addresses limitations of legacy cloud platforms for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks backlash among developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring stunt featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of applicants and successfully recruited talented engineers.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Top university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving explicit pornography and malicious content due to poor subdomain record-keeping. Scammers exploited weak administrative practices affecting hundreds of subdomains across at least 34 universities.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption to protect scrambled files against quantum computer attacks. This marks the first confirmed ransomware family using post-quantum cryptography standards.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core to fix CVE-2026-40372, a high-severity vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges on Linux and macOS devices through cryptographic signature verification flaws.

articles.title: The missing step between hype and profit
articles.summary: The article discusses the gap between AI hype and practical outcomes, referencing an anti-AI protest flyer that humorously highlights the missing 'Step 2' between AI development and tangible benefits.

articles.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
articles.summary: Enterprises struggle with poor data infrastructure as the main barrier to AI adoption. Organizations need unified, governed data systems to deploy AI effectively at scale.

articles.title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models
articles.summary: DeepSeek released V4, a new AI model that processes longer prompts efficiently and matches closed-source competitors from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. It's optimized for Huawei's Ascend chips, testing China's independence from Nvidia.

articles.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
articles.summary: OpenAI's Privacy Filter enables developers to build scalable web applications while protecting user data. The tool filters sensitive information before processing, ensuring compliance and security.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window that enables AI agents to process and utilize vast amounts of information effectively, advancing practical applications of long-context AI systems.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Guide on integrating Transformers.js library into Chrome extensions for running machine learning models directly in browsers without server dependencies.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology. The conversation explores emerging trends and upcoming developments in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creative output. These AI solutions enable companies to streamline content creation and enhance imaginative campaigns.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for sustainable farming innovation.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open. This hands-on program teaches developers to build and deploy AI agents.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered organization tips for decluttering your home and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox management, and seasonal chore planning.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs are specialized hardware accelerators designed to handle increasingly demanding AI workloads efficiently. A new video demonstrates how these processors power advanced machine learning applications.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new approach for distributed AI training that improves resilience and efficiency. The method allows independent local training followed by periodic synchronization, reducing communication overhead.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind has partnered with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technology. These collaborations aim to accelerate AI transformation across industries.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while Mamba 3 and attention residuals were also discussed.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time by boosting photorealism. The technology has potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Google warns malicious web pages are poisoning AI agents
articles.summary: Google researchers warn that malicious web pages are using indirect prompt injections to hijack enterprise AI agents. Hidden instructions embedded in HTML code execute when AI systems scrape pages for information.

articles.title: Why AI agents need interaction infrastructure
articles.summary: Enterprises need interaction infrastructure to manage autonomous AI agents operating across corporate networks. Band, a startup, raised $17M to build a dedicated layer governing how independent AI agents coordinate and operate.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to interpret volatile market behavior, which presents unique challenges and opportunities due to unpredictable price movements and non-repetitive patterns.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by improving target localization, self-localization, and path planning.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating their own training data and updating weights through learned self-editing via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals Claude Code inadvertently leaks sensitive API keys to public code repositories, exposing credentials through package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeover attacks, potentially creating a supply chain nightmare.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster, concise answers reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a critical tradeoff between speed and generalization capability.

articles.title: Samsung Wallet just got a travel feature that I hope Google Wallet copies ASAP
articles.summary: Samsung Wallet introduces a new travel feature exclusive to Galaxy users, a capability currently absent from Android's native Google Wallet.

articles.title: 6 MacOS settings I immediately change on every new Mac - and why
articles.summary: The article discusses six essential macOS settings that should be changed immediately on new Macs to improve user experience and functionality beyond the default configuration.

articles.title: I used a $4 timer to reboot my router, and it actually made my internet faster
articles.summary: A $4 timer device successfully improved internet speed by automating router reboots. This simple, inexpensive solution proved more effective than manual reboot methods.

articles.title: Text Summarization with Scikit-LLM
articles.summary: Scikit-LLM enables text summarization using machine learning techniques integrated with the popular scikit-learn library, simplifying NLP tasks for developers.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents with local small language models is now accessible to everyone, not just large tech companies. This democratizes AI development and enables more developers to create custom agents.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular framework for serving machine learning models due to its lightweight design, speed, and ease of use. It simplifies the process of training, serving, and deploying Scikit-learn models efficiently.

articles.title: A Career in Data Is Not Always a Straight Line, and That’s Okay
articles.summary: Data science careers don't follow linear paths. Flexibility is crucial, and professionals must balance leveraging AI agents with maintaining critical thinking skills.

articles.title: How Spreadsheets Quietly Cost Supply Chains Millions
articles.summary: Spreadsheets create costly inefficiencies in supply chain planning as forecast changes ripple through multiple teams, causing retailers to lose millions in the gap between sales and store operations.

articles.title: Comparing Explicit Measures to Calculation Groups in Tabular Models
articles.summary: The article discusses using calculation groups with UDFs as an alternative to explicit measures in tabular models, exploring which approach report creators should adopt.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the new system.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ titles, improving game discovery and helping gamers find content across their cloud libraries with enhanced performance.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: Scientists are developing new methods to better understand the early universe's formation and characteristics. Advanced AI techniques help analyze complex cosmological data.

articles.title: Build Strands Agents with SageMaker AI models and MLflow
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI enables enterprises to build AI agents with greater control over performance, cost, compliance, and infrastructure compared to managed FM services, while integrating with orchestration frameworks.

articles.title: How Popsa used Amazon Nova to inspire customers with personalised title suggestions
articles.summary: Popsa uses Amazon Nova to generate personalized photo book title suggestions, leveraging AI to automate design creation and enhance user experience across 50+ countries.

articles.title: Building Workforce AI Agents with Visier and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Visier and Amazon Quick integrate workforce intelligence with internal policies to enable better employee decision-making by connecting fragmented organizational data sources.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models supporting efficient million-token context inference on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. V4-Pro features 1.6T parameters with 49B active parameters for advanced AI applications.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring code refactoring, allowing organizations to train AI models across distributed data sources while maintaining data privacy and compliance.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated over 600,000 lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle competition, demonstrating how AI-assisted coding enables rapid iteration in machine learning.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study explores how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward ads across social media platforms, examining people's relationship with advertising on various channels.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough developments in cutting-edge XR solutions.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic state exploration, and improved gradient signals for vision-based control.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, aiming to make AI systems more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish between objects using noisy data and noise models.
